method_atom: A strong, externally imposed background field is used as a controlled probe of a medium: a sensitive low-energy signal traversing the field can reveal hypothesized weakly-coupled hidden degrees of freedom. Their coupling to the probe is captured by low-energy effective descriptions (effective actions) that parameterize the induced signatures, and purpose-built configurations exploit those signatures to discriminate among competing microscopic hypotheses and extract underlying parameters.
object_atom: The electromagnetized quantum vacuum probed by optical photons in strong electromagnetic fields, used to search for new light, weakly interacting particle-physics degrees of freedom as a complement to accelerator-driven experiments.
brief_reason: The background-field-as-probe plus effective-action parameterization used to detect and discriminate weakly-coupled hidden components is the transferable method, while the electromagnetic vacuum, optical photons, and light hidden particles are the physics-specific object.
